And again, Isaiah says <sup>[ 11:10]</sup>, “A root <sup>[i.e., a descendant]</sup> from Jesse will appear <sup>[i.e., Christ]</sup>, and He will rise up to rule over the nations, and the Gentiles will place their hope in Him.”
May God, <sup>[the source]</sup> of hope, fill you with complete joy and peace as you believe <sup>[in Christ]</sup>, so that you may have an abundance of hope in the power of the Holy Spirit.
And I, myself, am convinced about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, and have been filled with all kinds of knowledge and are capable of counseling one another.
But I have written to you quite boldly in some places to remind you <sup>[of certain things]</sup>, because of the favor shown to me by God,

in appointing me to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles. I am serving like a priest in preaching God’s good news, so that the Gentiles might be like an acceptable sacrifice <sup>[to God]</sup>, dedicated by the Holy Spirit.
Therefore, as I have fellowship with Christ Jesus, I am proud of my service to God.
For I will not dare to speak about anything, except what Christ has done through me in bringing about the obedience of the Gentiles. He accomplished this by my message and my deeds,

in the power demonstrated by <sup>[miraculous]</sup> signs and wonders, <sup>[produced]</sup> by the power of the Holy Spirit. So, I have completed preaching the good news about Christ from Jerusalem, all the way around <sup>[the area]</sup> to Illyricum <sup>[Note: This country was located northwest of Greece and in present-day Croatia]</sup>.
Now it has been my ambition to preach the good news at places where Christ’s name has never been heard, so that I would not be building on someone else’s foundation.

But, as it is written <sup>[Isa. 52:15]</sup>, “Those people who had not been told about Him will see <sup>[the truth]</sup>, and those who have not heard <sup>[the message]</sup> will understand <sup>[it]</sup>.” <sup>[Note: Paul here applies a Messianic prediction to his ambition of doing pioneer evangelism]</sup>.
This is why I have been so often hindered from coming to you.
But now, since I do not have any more <sup>[new]</sup> places <sup>[to preach]</sup> in this area, and have been wanting to visit you for many years,
I am hoping to see you when I travel to Spain. And I also hope to receive help from you in getting there <sup>[i.e., to Spain]</sup>, after enjoying a brief visit with you.
But for now, I am on my way to Jerusalem to minister to <sup>[the needs of]</sup> God’s holy people there.
For <sup>[the Christians in]</sup> Macedonia and Achaia <sup>[Note: These were provinces in Greece]</sup> thought it was a good idea to take up an offering for the poor saints at Jerusalem.

For they thought it was a good idea, and <sup>[besides]</sup> they were indebted to those Jewish Christians. For if the Gentiles <sup>[have]</sup> shared in the spiritual blessings of the Jews, <sup>[then]</sup> they owe it to those Jews to help meet their material needs.
So, when I have completed this task, and have guaranteed <sup>[the safe delivery of]</sup> this offering to them, I will visit you on my way to Spain.
And I know that when I arrive there <sup>[in Rome]</sup>, I will be coming with an abundance of Christ’s blessings <sup>[to give to you. See 1:11-12]</sup>.
Now I urge you, brothers, through our Lord Jesus Christ <sup>[See 12:1]</sup>, and through the Holy Spirit’s love <sup>[in us]</sup>, that you people join me in fervently praying to God for me

to be rescued from those who are disobedient in Judea <sup>[i.e., unbelieving Jews]</sup>. And <sup>[pray]</sup> that my service for Jerusalem <sup>[i.e., the offering for poor people. See verses 25-26]</sup> will be acceptable to God’s people there.
And pray that I will <sup>[be able to]</sup> come to you joyfully, if it is God’s will, and have a refreshing visit with you.
Now may the God of peace be with all of you. May it be so.
I recommend to you our sister Phoebe, who is a servant of the church at Cenchrea. <sup>[Note: This was a seaport town about seven miles east of Corinth]</sup>.

Welcome her <sup>[as someone to fellowship]</sup> in the Lord, in a way that God’s people should. And you people should help her in whatever way she needs you, for she herself has helped many people, including me.
I send greetings to Priscilla and Aquila, my fellow-workers in <sup>[the service of]</sup> Christ Jesus.
They risked their own lives for me, so not only I, but also all the Gentile churches <sup>[i.e., people converted from among the Gentiles]</sup> are grateful to them <sup>[for this]</sup>.

I also send greetings to the church that meets in their house. Greetings to my dear friend Epenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in Asia <sup>[Note: This was a province in the western part of present-day Turkey]</sup>.
Greetings to Mary, who worked very hard for you <sup>[i.e., she served the church in Rome]</sup>.

Greetings to Andronicus and Junias, my fellow-Jews <sup>[or possibly actual relatives]</sup> who were in prison with me. These <sup>[men]</sup> are considered outstanding by the apostles, and were Christians before I was <sup>[converted]</sup>.
Greetings to Ampliatus, my dear friend in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord.
Greetings to Urbanus, our fellow-worker in <sup>[the service of]</sup> Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
Greetings to Apelles, <sup>[who was tested and]</sup> approved in <sup>[the service of]</sup> Christ. Greetings to members of the family of Aristobulus.
Greetings to Herodion, my fellow-Jew. <sup>[See verse 7]</sup>. Greetings to those members of the family of Narcissus who are Christians.
Greetings to Tryphena and Tryphosa, who are workers in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord and to Persis, my dear friend who worked hard in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord.
Greetings to Rufus, an outstanding servant in <sup>[the service of]</sup> the Lord, and his mother, who has been a mother to me also.
Greetings to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas and the <sup>[other]</sup> brothers who are with them.
Greetings to Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all <sup>[the others]</sup> of God’s people who are with them.

Greet one another with a holy kiss <sup>[i.e., to signify affection, yet without sensuality]</sup>. All of the churches of Christ <sup>[i.e., which Paul established or preached in]</sup> send you their greetings.

Now I urge you, brothers, watch out for those who are causing divisions and occasions for falling <sup>[away from God over matters]</sup> contrary to the teaching you have learned, and withdraw from them <sup>[i.e., stop listening to their false teaching]</sup>.
For such men are not serving our Lord Christ but their own <sup>[unspiritual]</sup> appetites. They are deceiving the minds of innocent people by their smooth talk and flattering words.

For everyone has heard about your obedience <sup>[to the truth]</sup>, and <sup>[because of this]</sup>, I am very happy over you. But I want you to be wise about what is good and innocent about what is evil.

And the God, who brings peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet <sup>[i.e., bring an end to the divisive work of the false teachers there]</sup>. May the unearned favor of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you <sup>[people]</sup>.
My fellow-worker Timothy sends his greetings to you, and so do my fellow-Jews Lucius, Jason and Sosipater. <sup>[See verse 7]</sup>.
I am Tertius, who is writing this letter <sup>[for Paul]</sup>, and I send you my greetings in <sup>[the fellowship of]</sup> the Lord.

Gaius, who is hosting me <sup>[i.e., Paul]</sup> and the whole church <sup>[here]</sup>, sends you his greetings. <sup>[Note: This was probably the Gaius who lived in Corinth (I Cor. 1:14) and was apparently both wealthy and generous]</sup>. Erastus, the city treasurer <sup>[of Corinth]</sup> sends his greetings to you, along with our brother Quartus.

Now may there be glory for ever, through Jesus Christ, to the only wise God, who is able to make you stand firm <sup>[in the faith]</sup>, according to the good news and proclamation of Jesus Christ. This message is the secret which was <sup>[eventually]</sup> revealed, after having been concealed for long ages. It is now being made known through the writings of the prophets, as commanded by the eternal God, to <sup>[people of]</sup> all the nations, in order to bring about their obedience to the faith <sup>[i.e., so they will believe and obey the truth]</sup>. May it be so.
